content.claim: Church-maintained and private 6- and 8-year gimnáziums operate outside the Klebelsberg Centre's direct governance authority, meaning national reform of admissions criteria, capacity, or curriculum would require separate negotiation with church and minority maintainers rather than unilateral state decree.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The proportion of 6/8-year gimnáziums under church versus state maintenance is not quantified
The legal mechanism for negotiating with church maintainers (bilateral agreement, statutory process, etc.) is not specified
No data on whether past reform attempts have succeeded or failed with church-maintained schools
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
